MINNE lab

Code & Data

Open-source code and datasets from MINNE Lab. We aim to publish data and code from our projects whenever possible.

Source code

GitHub profile

rodrigomorenokth

The lab's primary GitHub account. Most repositories we publish live here; additional codebases can be made available on request.

Open on GitHub →

Notable repositories

  • Diffusion MRI

    Generalized Mean Intercept Length Tensor

    A generalisation of the mean-intercept-length tensor for anisotropy estimation in orientation-distribution data.

  • Vascular imaging

    Vesselness using the Ring Pattern Detector

    Vesselness filter built on the ring-pattern detector for robust extraction of tubular structures in medical images.

  • Tensor analysis

    Efficient Tensor Voting

    Fast implementation of tensor voting for perceptual grouping and structure inference.

  • Bone microarchitecture

    Classification of Trabeculae

    Classifier for trabecular microarchitecture in high-resolution CT of bone.

  • Geometry

    Distance Between Sets of Points

    Reference implementation of distance measures between discrete point sets.

Other codebases are available on request or will be published on GitHub soon.

Datasets

Open dataset

Synthetic Healthy Brain Aging MRIs with Segmentation Masks

A collection of 6,448 synthetic T1-weighted MRI scans simulating longitudinal brain aging, each with a corresponding segmentation mask. Generated using deep generative models to support research in neuroimaging and healthy aging.

Modality
T1-weighted MRI (synthetic)
Resolution
160 × 160 × 192 voxels, 1 × 1 × 1 mm
Volume
6,448 scans, 52.4 GB
DOI
10.23698/aida/synthetic/shbamri
Access
AIDA Data Hub →
Citation

Jingru Fu, Antonios Tzortzakakis, José Barroso, Eric Westman, Daniel Ferreira, Rodrigo Moreno (2023). Synthetic healthy brain aging MRIs with segmentation masks. doi:10.23698/aida/synthetic/shbamri

Related publication: Fu, J. et al. Fast 3D image generation for healthy brain aging using diffeomorphic registration. doi:10.1002/hbm.26165

MINNE lab

Research in biomedical imaging and deep learning, at KTH Royal Institute of Technology.
